music-starter.opentdb.ac91093737d000eb.explanation: The familiar Nokia Tune comes from a phrase in Francisco Tárrega’s 1902 guitar piece Gran Vals.
music-starter.opentdb.ac91093737d000eb.details: Nokia adopted the phrase for its phones in the 1990s, turning a few measures of classical guitar music into one of the world’s best-known ringtones.

music-starter.opentdb.42ae9a0524fbf229.explanation: “Some people call me the space cowboy” opens Steve Miller Band’s 1973 hit The Joker.
music-starter.opentdb.42ae9a0524fbf229.details: The song also supplied the much-discussed word “pompatus” and became the title track of the band’s eighth studio album.

music-starter.opentdb.cfbf55963f32bc98.explanation: Belinda Carlisle sings that “Heaven is a place on Earth” in her 1987 hit.
music-starter.opentdb.cfbf55963f32bc98.details: The song topped the Billboard Hot 100 and became Carlisle’s signature solo recording after her years with the Go-Go’s.

music-starter.opentdb.52852fc83856b81b.explanation: Desiigner’s opening lyric is “I got broads in Atlanta.”
music-starter.opentdb.52852fc83856b81b.details: Panda reached number one in the United States in 2016 and prominently samples the voice and production style associated with trap music.

music-starter.opentdb.4c3f6b1d7d39f24c.explanation: Psy’s Gangnam Style originated in South Korea.
music-starter.opentdb.4c3f6b1d7d39f24c.details: Its title refers to Seoul’s affluent Gangnam district. The 2012 video helped take K-pop worldwide and was YouTube’s first to reach one billion views.

music-starter.opentdb.9daaf96604abf30c.explanation: Nirvana’s best-known lineup was the trio of Kurt Cobain, Krist Novoselic, and Dave Grohl.
music-starter.opentdb.9daaf96604abf30c.details: The band used several drummers earlier in its history, but the classic three-member lineup recorded Nevermind and In Utero.

music-starter.opentdb.f6063b5d2eb9aa32.explanation: A standard cello has four strings, tuned C–G–D–A from lowest to highest.
music-starter.opentdb.f6063b5d2eb9aa32.details: They are tuned in perfect fifths, like the viola but one octave lower, giving the cello its broad bass-to-tenor range.

music-starter.opentdb.ad7821fa48b57e84.explanation: Daft Punk released four studio albums: Homework, Discovery, Human After All, and Random Access Memories.
music-starter.opentdb.ad7821fa48b57e84.details: The French duo also made the Tron: Legacy soundtrack, but soundtrack and live releases are not counted as studio albums.

music-starter.opentdb.99ec9ef78d2dd310.explanation: Kanye West interrupted Taylor Swift’s acceptance speech at the 2009 MTV Video Music Awards.
music-starter.opentdb.99ec9ef78d2dd310.details: Swift had won Best Female Video for You Belong with Me when West took the microphone to praise Beyoncé’s nominated video.

music-starter.opentdb.1bd92ed6da39097c.explanation: Sting primarily played bass guitar while singing for the Police.
music-starter.opentdb.1bd92ed6da39097c.details: His melodic bass lines are central to songs such as Walking on the Moon, Roxanne, and Every Breath You Take.

music-starter.opentdb.8396a52ba8ec893d.explanation: The British Invasion transformed American popular music during the 1960s.
music-starter.opentdb.8396a52ba8ec893d.details: The Beatles’ 1964 U.S. breakthrough was followed by acts including the Rolling Stones, the Who, the Kinks, and the Animals.

music-starter.opentdb.7cf906afa79eb7a9.explanation: The Weeknd’s Starboy features and was co-produced by Daft Punk.
music-starter.opentdb.7cf906afa79eb7a9.details: The collaborators also worked together on I Feel It Coming, another track from the 2016 album Starboy.

music-starter.opentdb.b22bac128f758cf2.explanation: YYZ is the airport code for Toronto Pearson International Airport.
music-starter.opentdb.b22bac128f758cf2.details: Rush translated the code’s Morse-code rhythm into the opening pattern of the instrumental YYZ, reflecting the band’s Toronto roots.

music-starter.opentdb.9d30033143881038.explanation: Bon Iver released 22, A Million in 2016.
music-starter.opentdb.9d30033143881038.details: The album moved beyond the project’s earlier folk sound with electronic processing, samples, saxophones, and symbol-heavy song titles.

music-starter.opentdb.6809589c171e3bc9.explanation: Weezer frontman Rivers Cuomo married Kyoko Ito in 2006.
music-starter.opentdb.6809589c171e3bc9.details: They met years earlier at one of Cuomo’s solo performances in Massachusetts and later married in Malibu.

music-starter.opentdb.08b461b7ed2b718a.explanation: Maroon 5’s 2014 album is titled V, the Roman numeral for five.
music-starter.opentdb.08b461b7ed2b718a.details: It was the group’s fifth studio album and includes Maps, Animals, and Sugar.

music-starter.opentdb.13311f789390d029.explanation: Stairway to Heaven appears on Led Zeppelin’s untitled fourth album, commonly called Led Zeppelin IV.
music-starter.opentdb.13311f789390d029.details: Released in 1971, the album also contains Black Dog, Rock and Roll, and When the Levee Breaks.

music-starter.opentdb.b9219db8d0bf91ab.explanation: Adele’s 25 was the world’s best-selling album of 2015.
music-starter.opentdb.b9219db8d0bf91ab.details: Led by Hello, it sold at an extraordinary pace despite arriving late in November and became the year’s dominant commercial release.

music-starter.opentdb.9f31bc1ab7fe93b0.explanation: Frank Ocean’s second studio album is Blonde.
music-starter.opentdb.9f31bc1ab7fe93b0.details: Released independently in August 2016, shortly after the visual album Endless, Blonde blends sparse R&B, pop, and experimental songwriting.

music-starter.opentdb.f8b49551f4084d46.explanation: Gangnam Style was uploaded to YouTube in 2012.
music-starter.opentdb.f8b49551f4084d46.details: The video debuted on July 15 and its viral dance and humor quickly carried Psy far beyond the Korean market.

music-starter.opentdb.e25f511bd3d3a5bf.explanation: The Beatles’ self-titled 1968 album has an almost entirely white cover and no photograph of the members.
music-starter.opentdb.e25f511bd3d3a5bf.details: Usually called the White Album, its minimalist sleeve was designed by pop artist Richard Hamilton as a contrast to Sgt. Pepper’s elaborate cover.

music-starter.opentdb.897921b56495aded.explanation: For Blood and Empire is a 2006 album by Pittsburgh punk band Anti-Flag.
music-starter.opentdb.897921b56495aded.details: Its politically charged songs address war, corporate power, and civil liberties, recurring themes throughout the band’s work.

music-starter.opentdb.4a3662b32e04d3c7.explanation: Fatman Scoop appears on Skrillex’s Recess, alongside Kill the Noise and Michael Angelakos.
music-starter.opentdb.4a3662b32e04d3c7.details: The track is the title song of Skrillex’s 2014 debut studio album.

music-starter.opentdb.ed3b8f4425030036.explanation: The Safety Dance was recorded by Men Without Hats, not Men at Work.
music-starter.opentdb.ed3b8f4425030036.details: Men at Work were the Australian group behind Down Under and Who Can It Be Now?; Men Without Hats were a Canadian new-wave band.

music-starter.opentdb.faf5b4692be3eedb.explanation: Death Grips released Year of the Snitch in 2018.
music-starter.opentdb.faf5b4692be3eedb.details: The experimental hip-hop trio announced the album through an eccentric promotional campaign and issued it as their sixth studio record.

music-starter.opentdb.f94e997cd0fbd58d.explanation: Vocalist Laura Brehm is featured on Feint’s electronic track Words.
music-starter.opentdb.f94e997cd0fbd58d.details: Brehm is known for melodic vocal collaborations with electronic producers, including several releases associated with Monstercat.

music-starter.opentdb.ece247dc63b4f337.explanation: Bob Dylan wrote and first recorded Make You Feel My Love.
music-starter.opentdb.ece247dc63b4f337.details: It appeared on his 1997 album Time Out of Mind and was later widely covered by Billy Joel, Garth Brooks, and Adele.

music-starter.opentdb.f518621c33bbfefb.explanation: GZA first performed under the name the Genius.
music-starter.opentdb.f518621c33bbfefb.details: A founding Wu-Tang Clan member, he retained the association in the expanded stage name GZA/Genius and released the acclaimed Liquid Swords.

music-starter.opentdb.b89a873823beb75a.explanation: The Beatles have long held the RIAA record as the highest-certified album act in the United States.
music-starter.opentdb.b89a873823beb75a.details: RIAA rankings count certified U.S. shipments and sales in award units, so this is a certification statistic rather than a worldwide sales estimate and can change with new audits.

music-starter.opentdb.2882e54af91546ed.explanation: Sammy Davis Jr. lost his left eye after a 1954 automobile accident.
music-starter.opentdb.2882e54af91546ed.details: He wore an eye patch during recovery and then a glass eye, returning to the stage only weeks after the crash.

music-starter.opentdb.d5e03fcd9c36bc0f.explanation: On Melancholy Hill appears on Gorillaz’ 2010 album Plastic Beach.
music-starter.opentdb.d5e03fcd9c36bc0f.details: Its warm synthesizer melody offsets lyrics about longing, and it became one of the virtual band’s most enduring songs.

music-starter.opentdb.00c8ae072d35b58c.explanation: The modern piano was invented in Italy by Bartolomeo Cristofori around 1700.
music-starter.opentdb.00c8ae072d35b58c.details: Cristofori’s hammer mechanism let players control volume through touch, unlike the plucked strings of the harpsichord.

music-starter.opentdb.725288c534ec5265.explanation: DJ Khaled built his career in Miami, where he became a radio DJ and producer.
music-starter.opentdb.725288c534ec5265.details: Born in New Orleans to Palestinian parents, he is associated musically with Miami rather than being a native of the city.

music-starter.opentdb.59ec11a6b0fa512c.explanation: Megadeth released Peace Sells... but Who’s Buying? in 1986.
music-starter.opentdb.59ec11a6b0fa512c.details: The album helped define thrash metal, with complex guitar work and politically skeptical lyrics led by its title track.

music-starter.opentdb.fac50a755983f71f.explanation: Johnny Cash covered Hurt, written by Nine Inch Nails’ Trent Reznor.
music-starter.opentdb.fac50a755983f71f.details: Cash’s stark 2002 recording and Mark Romanek’s video reframed the industrial-rock song as a reflection on age, memory, and loss.

music-starter.opentdb.368f85e4d205b3e2.explanation: Moby achieved worldwide success with his 1999 album Play.
music-starter.opentdb.368f85e4d205b3e2.details: Every track was eventually licensed for film, television, or advertising, helping its blend of electronic music and archival blues and gospel vocals reach a huge audience.

music-starter.opentdb.36f4d1f940ac0ce7.explanation: Paul McCartney is barefoot on the Abbey Road cover.
music-starter.opentdb.36f4d1f940ac0ce7.details: The photograph shows the four Beatles crossing Abbey Road outside EMI Studios; the bare feet later fed the fanciful “Paul is dead” conspiracy theory.

music-starter.opentdb.92db457dcb0b5b80.explanation: Rick Astley released Never Gonna Give You Up in 1987.
music-starter.opentdb.92db457dcb0b5b80.details: The Stock Aitken Waterman production topped charts internationally and decades later became the basis of the internet prank known as rickrolling.

music-starter.opentdb.4f208aa8cb09c620.explanation: John Cage’s 4′33″ contains no intentionally played notes—the performers remain silent.
music-starter.opentdb.4f208aa8cb09c620.details: The piece is not literally soundless: coughs, movement, and ambient noise become the music, directing attention to sounds listeners normally ignore.

music-starter.opentdb.15d0d9b52623403e.explanation: I’m Gonna Be (500 Miles) peaked at number three on the U.S. Billboard Hot 100 in 1993.
music-starter.opentdb.15d0d9b52623403e.details: The Proclaimers had released it in 1988, but its use in the film Benny & Joon drove its later American success.

music-starter.opentdb.734bda2337db48e2.explanation: Twin Size Mattress is by the New Jersey indie-rock band the Front Bottoms.
music-starter.opentdb.734bda2337db48e2.details: Released on Talon of the Hawk in 2013, it became one of the band’s most recognizable emotionally candid songs.

music-starter.opentdb.c4e0d9aa6bb7300f.explanation: September famously asks whether you remember “the 21st night of September.”
music-starter.opentdb.c4e0d9aa6bb7300f.details: Earth, Wind & Fire released the song in 1978. Co-writer Allee Willis later said the date was chosen largely because it sang well.

music-starter.opentdb.9de33fbef6079425.explanation: French producer Madeon became famous for performing mashups on a Novation Launchpad.
music-starter.opentdb.9de33fbef6079425.details: His 2011 Pop Culture video triggered samples from dozens of songs live and went viral while he was still a teenager.

music-starter.opentdb.61ff590488bb0caf.explanation: Madeon’s 2015 debut studio album is Adventure.
music-starter.opentdb.61ff590488bb0caf.details: The album includes collaborations with Passion Pit, Kyan, Mark Foster, and Aquilo and mixes electro-house with polished pop songwriting.

music-starter.opentdb.d2578644e4fe7265.explanation: Justice released the studio album Woman in 2016.
music-starter.opentdb.d2578644e4fe7265.details: The French duo’s third album combines their distorted electronic style with disco, funk, choirs, and live instrumentation.

music-starter.opentdb.c84e64d18222d3d9.prompt: What is the relationship among the members of the American rock band Kings of Leon?
music-starter.opentdb.c84e64d18222d3d9.explanation: Kings of Leon consists of three Followill brothers and their cousin.
music-starter.opentdb.c84e64d18222d3d9.details: Caleb, Nathan, and Jared Followill are brothers; lead guitarist Matthew Followill is their cousin. The band name honors their grandfather Leon.

music-starter.opentdb.088ccd509eeea772.explanation: Kanye West appears on ScHoolboy Q’s THat Part.
music-starter.opentdb.088ccd509eeea772.details: The single was released in 2016 and appears on Blank Face LP; its unusual capitalization matches ScHoolboy Q’s stylized use of the letter H.

music-starter.opentdb.8b94b7b1e7e9e2bf.explanation: Bon Iver’s debut album is For Emma, Forever Ago.
music-starter.opentdb.8b94b7b1e7e9e2bf.details: Justin Vernon largely recorded it in a remote Wisconsin cabin; its intimate falsetto-led folk sound launched the Bon Iver project.

music-starter.opentdb.86f79a559f0e037c.explanation: Raekwon’s debut solo album is Only Built 4 Cuban Linx....
music-starter.opentdb.86f79a559f0e037c.details: Released in 1995 and heavily produced by RZA, the album’s cinematic crime narratives made it a landmark of East Coast hip-hop.

music-starter.opentdb.828380d16e56d43d.explanation: Martin Shkreli bought Wu-Tang Clan’s unique album Once Upon a Time in Shaolin for about $2 million.
music-starter.opentdb.828380d16e56d43d.details: Only one physical copy was produced. It was later forfeited after Shkreli’s conviction and sold by the U.S. government.

music-starter.opentdb.d92f5d6e3635fad8.prompt: What year was the Red Hot Chili Peppers album Californication released?
music-starter.opentdb.d92f5d6e3635fad8.explanation: The Red Hot Chili Peppers released Californication in 1999.
music-starter.opentdb.d92f5d6e3635fad8.details: The album marked guitarist John Frusciante’s return and includes Scar Tissue, Otherside, and the title track.

music-starter.opentdb.eda52e36629c9581.explanation: The Beatles released their debut LP Please Please Me in 1963.
music-starter.opentdb.eda52e36629c9581.details: Most of the album was recorded in a famously long session at EMI Studios on February 11, just over a month before its U.K. release.

music-starter.opentdb.23d68b3eb0c97e7b.explanation: Lorde curated the soundtrack for The Hunger Games: Mockingjay – Part 1.
music-starter.opentdb.23d68b3eb0c97e7b.details: She also recorded its lead single Yellow Flicker Beat and assembled contributions from artists including Chvrches, Charli XCX, and Stromae.

music-starter.opentdb.212ef72166ecd3f7.explanation: Bread recorded Baby I’m-a Want You, Everything I Own, and The Guitar Man.
music-starter.opentdb.212ef72166ecd3f7.details: David Gates led the soft-rock group, whose melodic ballads were major U.S. hits in the early 1970s.

music-starter.opentdb.7daab53658215e00.explanation: The Eagles had 1975 hits with One of These Nights and Lyin’ Eyes.
music-starter.opentdb.7daab53658215e00.details: Both appear on One of These Nights; the title track reached number one, while Lyin’ Eyes won a Grammy.

music-starter.opentdb.0071d6091178d6cf.explanation: MGMT released Electric Feel, Kids, and Time to Pretend.
music-starter.opentdb.0071d6091178d6cf.details: All three tracks appear on the duo’s 2007 debut album Oracular Spectacular and helped define late-2000s psychedelic indie pop.

music-starter.opentdb.83478d7cb7f649fb.explanation: Paul McCartney wrote and sang Why Don’t We Do It in the Road?, with Ringo Starr on drums.
music-starter.opentdb.83478d7cb7f649fb.details: McCartney said the blunt song was inspired by seeing two monkeys mate in the street while the Beatles were in India.

music-starter.opentdb.e07d1decea3c6b4e.explanation: Dave Navarro recorded with the Red Hot Chili Peppers on One Hot Minute.
music-starter.opentdb.e07d1decea3c6b4e.details: The Jane’s Addiction guitarist joined after John Frusciante’s first departure and was the band’s guitarist from 1993 to 1998.

music-starter.opentdb.f2a34c8ee230af54.details: The heavy-metal band formed in Falun, Sweden, and is known for songs about military history; the other listed acts in the question are Finnish.

music-starter.opentdb.38cc5f05c7bd669b.explanation: Pink Floyd is the oldest of the listed bands, having formed in London in 1965.
music-starter.opentdb.38cc5f05c7bd669b.details: Its earliest lineup centered on Syd Barrett; David Gilmour joined in 1967, before Barrett’s departure.

music-starter.opentdb.8503dc25d72aa1bf.explanation: Phi Cypher is not a member of Gorillaz.
music-starter.opentdb.8503dc25d72aa1bf.details: The virtual band’s core fictional lineup is 2-D, Murdoc Niccals, Noodle, and Russel Hobbs.

music-starter.opentdb.b726f08f0f653c7b.explanation: The Wall is a Pink Floyd album, not a Beatles album.
music-starter.opentdb.b726f08f0f653c7b.details: Pink Floyd released the rock opera in 1979; the Beatles’ catalog includes similarly iconic titles such as Revolver, Abbey Road, and Let It Be.

music-starter.opentdb.da52b113850bcf9e.explanation: Don’t Ever Play Yourself is a DJ Khaled catchphrase, not one of his album titles.
music-starter.opentdb.da52b113850bcf9e.details: His actual albums use related motivational slogans, including We the Best, Major Key, and Grateful.

music-starter.opentdb.ab5b00d4e8beee01.explanation: Producer Dan the Automator is one of the three principal artists behind Deltron 3030.
music-starter.opentdb.ab5b00d4e8beee01.details: He joined rapper Del the Funky Homosapien and turntablist Kid Koala to create the project’s futuristic concept album.

music-starter.opentdb.85cf2cf5d0339f45.explanation: Pink Floyd’s The Wall was adapted into the 1982 film Pink Floyd – The Wall.
music-starter.opentdb.85cf2cf5d0339f45.details: Directed by Alan Parker and starring Bob Geldof, the surreal film builds a visual narrative around the album’s alienated rock-star protagonist.

music-starter.opentdb.386bd2ce42260009.explanation: Brittany Murphy performed Queen’s Somebody to Love in Happy Feet.
music-starter.opentdb.386bd2ce42260009.details: She voiced Gloria in the 2006 animated film; the performance accompanies the penguins’ song-driven courtship story.

music-starter.opentdb.96b3dbd08e8be117.explanation: Aloe Blacc supplied the lead vocal on Avicii’s Wake Me Up.
music-starter.opentdb.96b3dbd08e8be117.details: The 2013 hit fused folk-style acoustic guitar and soul singing with electronic dance production; Blacc co-wrote the lyrics.

music-starter.opentdb.abe206be0b3a7c61.explanation: Ascent is not a song by the electronic producer TheFatRat.
music-starter.opentdb.abe206be0b3a7c61.details: TheFatRat’s catalog includes tracks such as Unity, Monody, and Windfall, known for bright melodies and gaming-video popularity.

music-starter.opentdb.bad6fe0a1472f496.explanation: Leo Sayer had the 1976 hit You Make Me Feel Like Dancing.
music-starter.opentdb.bad6fe0a1472f496.details: The disco-pop single reached number one in the United States and won the Grammy for Best R&B Song.

music-starter.opentdb.9e9863b88b10d501.explanation: The Tornados topped U.S. and U.K. charts with the instrumental Telstar.
music-starter.opentdb.9e9863b88b10d501.details: Joe Meek produced the space-age 1962 recording, named for the communications satellite launched that year.

music-starter.opentdb.8885653d45290d20.explanation: Andrew Gold recorded the 1970s hits Lonely Boy and Never Let Her Slip Away.
music-starter.opentdb.8885653d45290d20.details: Gold was also a prolific multi-instrumentalist and arranger who worked closely with Linda Ronstadt.

music-starter.opentdb.6b31b64051aca868.explanation: Daniel Johns was Silverchair’s lead singer and guitarist.
music-starter.opentdb.6b31b64051aca868.details: The Australian trio became internationally successful as teenagers with the 1995 debut Frogstomp and its single Tomorrow.

music-starter.opentdb.66b652509186b6c0.explanation: Billy Corgan is the lead singer, principal songwriter, and guitarist of the Smashing Pumpkins.
music-starter.opentdb.66b652509186b6c0.details: His distinctive voice and layered guitar production shape albums including Siamese Dream and Mellon Collie and the Infinite Sadness.

music-starter.opentdb.e6bc230bd2f61d0a.explanation: Eddie Van Halen played the guitar solo on Michael Jackson’s Beat It.
music-starter.opentdb.e6bc230bd2f61d0a.details: He recorded the blazing solo as a favor and was not paid; the crossover helped the Thriller track bridge pop and hard rock.

music-starter.opentdb.e8a7a9d3c4b936dc.explanation: Chino Moreno is the lead vocalist of Deftones.
music-starter.opentdb.e8a7a9d3c4b936dc.details: His shifts between melodic singing, whispers, and screams are central to the Sacramento band’s atmospheric alternative-metal sound.

music-starter.opentdb.d222b0b1f39da8c7.prompt: In Panic! at the Disco’s song Northern Downpour, which lyric follows “I know the world’s a broken bone”?
music-starter.opentdb.d222b0b1f39da8c7.canonicalAnswer: So melt your headaches, call it home
music-starter.opentdb.d222b0b1f39da8c7.explanation: The next line is “So melt your headaches, call it home.”
music-starter.opentdb.d222b0b1f39da8c7.details: Northern Downpour appears on the band’s 2008 album Pretty. Odd., whose ornate pop and psychedelic influences marked a change from their debut.

music-starter.opentdb.ce7b2db257168c48.explanation: Eleanor Rigby appears on the Beatles’ 1966 album Revolver.
music-starter.opentdb.ce7b2db257168c48.details: Its string-octet arrangement, written by George Martin, uses no conventional rock-band instrumentation and supports Paul McCartney’s story of loneliness.

music-starter.opentdb.636a61913fb31cc7.explanation: Panic! at the Disco released Pray for the Wicked on June 22, 2018.
music-starter.opentdb.636a61913fb31cc7.details: The album includes Say Amen (Saturday Night) and High Hopes, which became the act’s biggest U.S. chart hit.

music-starter.opentdb.4df62cb9d9770859.explanation: Pete Townshend collaborated with Pink Floyd guitarist David Gilmour at Brixton Academy in 1985.
music-starter.opentdb.4df62cb9d9770859.details: Gilmour performed in Townshend’s short-lived ensemble Deep End, whose concerts produced a live album and video.

music-starter.opentdb.02c907427c2e6838.explanation: The Velvet Underground & Nico sold poorly on release but became enormously influential.
music-starter.opentdb.02c907427c2e6838.details: The 1967 album paired Lou Reed’s unflinching songs with avant-garde textures and Andy Warhol’s banana cover; its reputation grew long after its initial chart failure.

music-starter.opentdb.edb5eef75a98876e.explanation: New Zealand singer Phillipa Brown performs as Ladyhawke.
music-starter.opentdb.edb5eef75a98876e.details: She took the name from the 1985 fantasy film and broke through with the synth-pop singles Paris Is Burning and My Delirium.

music-starter.opentdb.a140dcdaa42d6384.explanation: Steps covered the Bee Gees song Tragedy in 1998.
music-starter.opentdb.a140dcdaa42d6384.details: The Bee Gees recorded it during the disco era for Spirits Having Flown; Steps paired their cover with Heartbeat as a double A-side.

music-starter.opentdb.c4af96fd4c434a45.explanation: Viktor Tsoi was the Soviet rock singer commemorated by Moscow’s Tsoi Wall.
music-starter.opentdb.c4af96fd4c434a45.details: As leader of the band Kino, he became an emblem of late-Soviet youth culture. Kino’s Gruppa krovi appears on the Grand Theft Auto IV soundtrack.

music-starter.opentdb.c79e60be45d413d4.explanation: Dire Straits released Money for Nothing in 1985.
music-starter.opentdb.c79e60be45d413d4.details: The Brothers in Arms single features Sting’s backing vocal and became famous for its early computer-animated music video.

music-starter.opentdb.1cb8979d8a0422a9.explanation: Min Yoongi, known as Suga of BTS and Agust D, was born in 1993.
music-starter.opentdb.1cb8979d8a0422a9.details: The South Korean rapper, songwriter, and producer was born on March 9 in Daegu.

music-starter.opentdb.03f2fa19a9e3c05b.explanation: “When are you gonna come down?” opens Elton John’s Goodbye Yellow Brick Road.
music-starter.opentdb.03f2fa19a9e3c05b.details: The Bernie Taupin lyric addresses someone abandoning a glamorous fantasy; the 1973 song is the title track of Elton John’s double album.

music-starter.opentdb.e05bb46ca0dd424a.explanation: The lyric “A rainy Lithuanian / Is dancing as an Indian” refers to Anthony Kiedis.
music-starter.opentdb.e05bb46ca0dd424a.details: It appears in R.E.M.’s E-Bow the Letter; Kiedis has Lithuanian ancestry through his mother’s family and Native American ancestry through his father.

music-starter.opentdb.8b56fadd83bb401a.explanation: Limitless Fury is not a Thousand Foot Krutch song.
music-starter.opentdb.8b56fadd83bb401a.details: The Canadian rock band has released similarly forceful titles such as Courtesy Call, War of Change, and Untraveled Road, making the invented name plausible.

music-starter.opentdb.4d8be2111b579803.prompt: Which of these artists did not feature on Gorillaz’ fifth studio album Humanz?
music-starter.opentdb.4d8be2111b579803.explanation: Snoop Dogg did not appear on Humanz.
music-starter.opentdb.4d8be2111b579803.details: Humanz has a large guest cast including Vince Staples, Grace Jones, Pusha T, Mavis Staples, and Benjamin Clementine; Snoop Dogg appeared on the earlier Gorillaz track Welcome to the World of the Plastic Beach.

music-starter.opentdb.8add098d071d5d3e.explanation: AC/DC’s Skies on Fire is not on the Suicide Squad soundtrack.
music-starter.opentdb.8add098d071d5d3e.details: The 2016 soundtrack instead mixes original and licensed tracks by artists such as Twenty One Pilots, Skrillex, Rick Ross, and Panic! at the Disco.

music-starter.opentdb.ca076e0ad4fbcf77.explanation: The Smashing Pumpkins released 1979 as a single in 1996.
music-starter.opentdb.ca076e0ad4fbcf77.details: Despite its title, the song looks back on Billy Corgan’s adolescence; it appears on Mellon Collie and the Infinite Sadness.

music-starter.opentdb.362b3d83e79c3abc.explanation: Rihanna provides vocals on Kanye West’s Famous.
music-starter.opentdb.362b3d83e79c3abc.details: The 2016 Life of Pablo track also samples Nina Simone’s Do What You Gotta Do and Sister Nancy’s Bam Bam.

music-starter.opentdb.2b0d41f42d508b6d.explanation: Macintosh Plus released Floral Shoppe in 2011.
music-starter.opentdb.2b0d41f42d508b6d.details: Vektroid’s album became a defining vaporwave release through its slowed samples, surreal Japanese-influenced artwork, and internet circulation.

music-starter.opentdb.995dfbab44992528.explanation: The Last Emperor performs Secret Wars Part 1.
music-starter.opentdb.995dfbab44992528.details: The track imagines comic-book superheroes battling famous rappers, showcasing the Philadelphia MC’s detailed storytelling and pop-culture references.

music-starter.opentdb.8bb0fd338faca1f6.explanation: Martin Chambers was not a member of the Smiths; he was the drummer for the Pretenders.
music-starter.opentdb.8bb0fd338faca1f6.details: The Smiths’ core lineup was Morrissey, Johnny Marr, Andy Rourke, and Mike Joyce.
